message:
feat: Phase 2 - Cell Scheduler for continuous batching

Implements the Cell Scheduler pattern for parallel inference:

- Cell struct: Represents an inference "thread" with its own seq_id
  in the KV cache, state machine (idle/thinking/waiting), and buffer
- Scheduler: Manages multiple Cells via Gather/Crunch/Dispatch loop
  - Gather: Collects pending tokens from all active cells into batch
  - Crunch: Runs single llama_decode() on the combined batch
  - Dispatch: Samples tokens, handles EOG, updates cell buffers
- KV Cache management: Per-cell memory isolation via seq_ids
- New CLI command: `zell batch` for parallel prompt processing

This enables one model to drive multiple "thinking threads"
simultaneously, with each Cell maintaining its own context.

files.patch:
@@ -42,22 +42,23 @@ zell version
 
 ```
 zell/
-├── build.zig        # Build configuration
+├── build.zig          # Build configuration
 ├── src/
-│   ├── main.zig     # CLI entry point
-│   └── llama.zig    # Zig wrapper for llama.cpp C API
+│   ├── main.zig       # CLI entry point
+│   ├── llama.zig      # Zig wrapper for llama.cpp C API
+│   └── scheduler.zig  # Cell Scheduler for continuous batching
 └── vendor/
-    └── llama.cpp/   # llama.cpp submodule
+    └── llama.cpp/     # llama.cpp submodule
 ```
 
 ## Roadmap
 
 - [x] Basic inference (load model, generate text)
+- [x] Continuous batching (multiple concurrent requests via Cell Scheduler)
 - [ ] KV cache snapshots (Wizer-style instant resume)
 - [ ] Library API with C header
 - [ ] lanceql integration (LoRA selection, RAG)
 - [ ] edgebox integration (tool execution)
-- [ ] Continuous batching (multiple concurrent requests)
 
 ## Part of the Stack
